content:
# Numbers Chapter 26 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 179-181 | **Source Image:** `NUM_026_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Manuscript:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0132-pa_0134

---

**1** And it was after the plague, and YHWH spoke to Moses and to Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying:

**2** "Take up the head of all the congregation of the Sons of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, by their fathers' houses, all who go out to war in Israel."

**3** And Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke to them in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho, saying:

**4** "From twenty years old and upward," as YHWH had commanded Moses. And the Sons of Israel who came out from the land of Egypt:

**5** Reuben, the firstborn of Israel -- the sons of Reuben: of Hanoch, the clan of the Hanochites; of Pallu, the clan of the Palluites;

**6** of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Carmi, the clan of the Carmites.

**7** These are the clans of the Reubenites, and their numbered ones were forty-three thousand and seven hundred and thirty.

**8** And the sons of Pallu: Eliab.

**9** And the sons of Eliab: Nemuel and Dathan and Abiram. These are the Dathan and Abiram, the called ones of the congregation, who contended against Moses and against Aaron in the congregation of Korah, when they contended against YHWH.

**10** And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them together with Korah, when that congregation died, when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a sign.

**11** But the sons of Korah did not die.

**12** The sons of Simeon by their clans: of Nemuel, the clan of the Nemuelites; of Jamin, the clan of the Jaminites; of Jachin, the clan of the Jachinites;

**13** of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites; of Shaul, the clan of the Shaulites.

**14** These are the clans of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand and two hundred.

**15** The sons of Gad by their clans: of Zephon, the clan of the Zephonites; of Haggi, the clan of the Haggites; of Shuni, the clan of the Shunites;

**16** of Ozni, the clan of the Oznites; of Eri, the clan of the Erites;

**17** of Arod, the clan of the Arodites; of Areli, the clan of the Arelites.

**18** These are the clans of the sons of Gad, according to their numbered ones: forty thousand and five hundred.

**19** The sons of Judah: Er and Onan. And Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.

**20** And the sons of Judah by their clans were: of Shelah, the clan of the Shelanites; of Perez, the clan of the Perezites; of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites.

**21** And the sons of Perez were: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Hamul, the clan of the Hamulites.

**22** These are the clans of Judah, according to their numbered ones: seventy-six thousand and five hundred.

**23** The sons of Issachar by their clans: of Tola, the clan of the Tolaites; of Puvah, the clan of the Punites;

**24** of Jashub, the clan of the Jashubites; of Shimron, the clan of the Shimronites.

**25** These are the clans of Issachar, according to their numbered ones: sixty-four thousand and three hundred.

**26** The sons of Zebulun by their clans: of Sered, the clan of the Seredites; of Elon, the clan of the Elonites; of Jahleel, the clan of the Jahleelites.

**27** These are the clans of the Zebulunites, according to their numbered ones: sixty thousand and five hundred.

**28** The sons of Joseph by their clans: Manasseh and Ephraim.

**29** The sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the clan of the Machirites. And Machir begot Gilead; of Gilead, the clan of the Gileadites.

**30** These are the sons of Gilead: of Iezer, the clan of the Iezerites; of Helek, the clan of the Helekites;

**31** and of Asriel, the clan of the Asrielites; and of Shechem, the clan of the Shechemites;

**32** and of Shemida, the clan of the Shemidaites; and of Hepher, the clan of the Hepherites.

**33** And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but only daughters; and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

**34** These are the clans of Manasseh, and their numbered ones were fifty-two thousand and seven hundred.

**35** These are the sons of Ephraim by their clans: of Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahites; of Becher, the clan of the Becherites; of Tahan, the clan of the Tahanites.

**36** And these are the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the clan of the Eranites.

**37** These are the clans of the sons of Ephraim, according to their numbered ones: thirty-two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph by their clans.

**38** The sons of Benjamin by their clans: of Bela, the clan of the Belaites; of Ashbel, the clan of the Ashbelites; of Ahiram, the clan of the Ahiramites;

**39** of Shephupham, the clan of the Shuphamites; of Hupham, the clan of the Huphamites.

**40** And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the clan of the Ardites; of Naaman, the clan of the Naamites.

**41** These are the sons of Benjamin by their clans, and their numbered ones were forty-five thousand and six hundred.

**42** These are the sons of Dan by their clans: of Shuham, the clan of the Shuhamites. These are the clans of Dan by their clans.

**43** All the clans of the Shuhamites, according to their numbered ones: sixty-four thousand and four hundred.

**44** The sons of Asher by their clans: of Imnah, the clan of the Imnites; of Ishvi, the clan of the Ishvites; of Beriah, the clan of the Beriites.

**45** Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the clan of the Heberites; of Malchiel, the clan of the Malchielites.

**46** And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah.

**47** These are the clans of the sons of Asher, according to their numbered ones: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

**48** The sons of Naphtali by their clans: of Jahzeel, the clan of the Jahzeelites; of Guni, the clan of the Gunites;

**49** of Jezer, the clan of the Jezerites; of Shillem, the clan of the Shillemites.

**50** These are the clans of Naphtali by their clans, and their numbered ones were forty-five thousand and four hundred.

**51** These are the numbered ones of the Sons of Israel: six hundred and one thousand and seven hundred and thirty.

**52** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**53** "To these the land shall be divided for an inheritance, according to the number of names."

**54** "To the greater you shall give the greater inheritance, and to the fewer you shall give the fewer inheritance; each according to its numbered ones shall its inheritance be given."

**55** "However, by lot shall the land be divided; according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit."

**56** "According to the lot shall its inheritance be divided, between the greater and the fewer."

**57** And these are the numbered ones of the Levites by their clans: of Gershon, the clan of the Gershonites; of Kohath, the clan of the Kohathites; of Merari, the clan of the Merarites.

**58** These are the clans of Levi: the clan of the Libnites, the clan of the Hebronites, the clan of the Mahlites, the clan of the Mushites, the clan of the Korahites. And Kohath begot Amram.

**59** And the name of the wife of Amram was Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bore to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram: Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister.

**60** And to Aaron were born Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

**61** And Nadab and Abihu died when they offered strange fire before YHWH.

**62** And their numbered ones were twenty-three thousand, every male from a month old and upward; for they were not numbered among the Sons of Israel, because no inheritance was given to them among the Sons of Israel.

**63** These are the ones numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who numbered the Sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho.

**64** And among these there was not a man from the numbered ones of Moses and Aaron the priest, who numbered the Sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.

**65** For YHWH had said of them, "Dying they shall die in the wilderness." And there was not left of them a man, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

Numbers 26 is the second great census chapter of the Torah, forming a deliberate structural parallel with Numbers 1. The three translators produced highly convergent renderings for the majority of this chapter, especially in the formulaic clan lists (vv. 5-7, 12-18, 20-27, 29-32, 34-50). The census formulae -- "of [Name], the clan of the [Name]-ites" -- are rendered identically or near-identically across all three translators in most tribal entries. The Leningrad Codex pages 179-181 are in good condition, with clear vocalization throughout; the Vaticanus confirms all census totals and the chapter's narrative structure. Where all three translators agree, the consensus follows without alteration. The clan names, census totals, and genealogical data are uniform across all three translations. Verses where all three translations are substantively identical include: vv. 6, 8, 12-13, 15-17, 20-21, 23-24, 26, 28, 30-32, 34, 36, 38-40, 43-45, 48-49, 52, 57-58, 60.

---

## Disagreement Log

### Verse 1
- **EMET:** "And-it-was after the-plague; and-said YHWH unto Moses and-unto Eleazar son of Aaron the-priest, saying:"
- **LOGOS:** "And it was after the plague, and YHWH spoke to Moses and to Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying,"
- **PNEUMA:** "After the plague had ended, YHWH spoke to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying:"
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA adds "had ended," which is interpretive expansion not in the Hebrew. EMET uses "said" for va-yomer while LOGOS/PNEUMA use "spoke." The Hebrew has va-yomer (said), but the verse also functions as a narrative introduction. SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's rendering as the formal baseline, using "spoke" (which captures the discourse-initiating force of the construction) and retaining "And it was" to preserve the Hebrew wayyiqtol opening.

### Verse 2
- **EMET:** "Lift up the-head of all the-congregation of the-Sons of Israel, from-a-son of twenty year and-upward, by-the-house of their-fathers, all going-out to-the-host in-Israel."
- **LOGOS:** "Take up the sum of all the congregation of the sons of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, by their fathers' houses, all who go out to war in Israel."
- **PNEUMA:** "Take a census of the entire community of the Sons of Israel -- everyone twenty years old or more, by their ancestral houses -- all who are fit for military service in Israel."
- **Resolution:** The Hebrew se'u et-rosh ("lift up the head") is the census idiom. EMET preserves the literal idiom; LOGOS renders it as "take up the sum"; PNEUMA interprets freely as "take a census." SOPHIA adopts "Take up the head" to preserve the distinctive Hebrew census idiom while remaining intelligible. "Going out to war" (LOGOS) is preferred over "fit for military service" (PNEUMA) and "going-out to-the-host" (EMET) as the balanced rendering of yotse' tsava'. PNEUMA's "ancestral houses" departs from the Hebrew le-veit avotam; SOPHIA retains "fathers' houses." PNEUMA's "entire community" replaces the Hebrew kol adat ("all the congregation"); SOPHIA retains "all the congregation."

### Verse 3
- **EMET:** "And-spoke Moses and-Eleazar the-priest them in-the-plains of Moab, by the-Jordan of Jericho, saying:"
- **LOGOS:** "And Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke to them in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,"
- **PNEUMA:** "So Moses and Eleazar the priest addressed them on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan opposite Jericho, saying:"
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses "addressed" (interpretive) and "opposite Jericho" for al Yarden Yerekho; EMET uses "of Jericho" (too literal); LOGOS uses "at Jericho." The Hebrew al means "upon/by/at" and Yarden Yerekho is a construct chain. "By the Jordan at Jericho" (LOGOS) is the most natural rendering. PNEUMA's "So" replaces "And" (va-yedabber); SOPHIA preserves the conjunction.

### Verse 4
- **EMET:** "'From-a-son of twenty year and-upward' -- as commanded YHWH Moses and-the-Sons of Israel, the-ones-going-out from-the-land of Egypt:"
- **LOGOS:** "'From twenty years old and upward,' as YHWH had commanded Moses and the sons of Israel who came out from the land of Egypt."
- **PNEUMA:** "'Count everyone twenty years old or more, just as YHWH commanded Moses.' These were the Sons of Israel who came out of the land of Egypt:"
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA restructures by adding "Count everyone" (not in the Hebrew) and splitting the verse into two sentences with different referents. EMET preserves the Hebrew syntax most closely. LOGOS balances syntax and readability. The Hebrew is syntactically compressed: the final clause "and the Sons of Israel who came out from the land of Egypt" serves as a transitional heading. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's structure but adds "And" before "the Sons of Israel" to preserve the Hebrew conjunction and the transitional function.

### Verse 5
- **EMET:** "Reuben, the-firstborn of Israel -- the-sons of Reuben: of-Hanoch, the-clan of the-Hanochite; of-Pallu, the-clan of the-Palluite;"
- **LOGOS:** "Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: the sons of Reuben -- Hanoch, the clan of the Hanochites; of Pallu, the clan of the Palluites;"
- **PNEUMA:** "Reuben was Israel's firstborn. The descendants of Reuben: from Hanoch, the Hanochite clan; from Pallu, the Palluite clan;"
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA restructures into two sentences and uses "descendants" instead of "sons" (benei). PNEUMA also uses "from" instead of "of" for the Hebrew le- prefix. EMET uses singular gentilic ("the Hanochite") while LOGOS/PNEUMA use plural. The Hebrew has the gentilic in singular form (ha-Khanokhi), but the clan name is collective. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's rendering with plural gentilics (standard English convention for clan names) and "the sons of" for benei. SOPHIA retains EMET/LOGOS's em-dash structure rather than PNEUMA's sentence break.

### Verse 7
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-Reubenite; and-were their-mustered-ones three and-forty thousand and-seven hundreds and-thirty."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the Reubenites, and their numbered ones were forty-three thousand and seven hundred and thirty."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the Reubenites. Their registered number was forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty."
- **Resolution:** EMET uses "mustered-ones" for pequdeihem; LOGOS uses "numbered ones"; PNEUMA uses "registered number." The Hebrew paqad means "to count/muster/number." SOPHIA adopts "numbered ones" (LOGOS) as the standard rendering throughout. PNEUMA uses past tense "were" for the demonstrative; EMET/LOGOS use "are" -- the Hebrew elleh supports present-tense rendering. PNEUMA splits into two sentences; SOPHIA preserves the single-sentence structure.

### Verse 8
- **EMET:** "And-the-sons of Pallu -- Eliab."
- **LOGOS:** "And the sons of Pallu: Eliab."
- **PNEUMA:** "And the son of Pallu was Eliab."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses singular "son" and adds "was." The Hebrew u-vnei (and-sons-of) is plural construct, though only one son is named. EMET/LOGOS preserve the Hebrew plural. SOPHIA follows the Hebrew plural form with LOGOS.

### Verse 9
- **EMET:** "And-the-sons of Eliab -- Nemuel and-Dathan and-Abiram. This Dathan and-Abiram, the-called-ones of the-congregation, who contended against Moses and-against Aaron in-the-congregation of Korah, when-they-contended against YHWH."
- **LOGOS:** "And the sons of Eliab: Nemuel and Dathan and Abiram. These are the Dathan and Abiram, the called ones of the congregation, who contended against Moses and against Aaron in the congregation of Korah, when they contended against YHWH."
- **PNEUMA:** "And the sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. These are the same Dathan and Abiram -- chosen representatives of the community -- who rebelled against Moses and Aaron as part of Korah's faction, when they defied YHWH."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA significantly reinterprets: "chosen representatives" for qeri'ei ha-edah ("called ones of the congregation"), "rebelled" for hittsu ("contended"), "Korah's faction" for "congregation of Korah," and "defied" for "contended against." SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS's more literal rendering of the Hebrew terms, adopting LOGOS's structure. The Hebrew qeri'ei ("called ones") is a passive participle best rendered literally.

### Verse 10
- **EMET:** "And-opened the-earth her-mouth and-swallowed them and-Korah, in-the-dying of the-congregation, when-devoured the-fire the-five and-two-hundred fifty men; and-they-became a-sign."
- **LOGOS:** "And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them together with Korah, when that congregation died, when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a sign."
- **PNEUMA:** "And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them along with Korah, when that faction perished -- when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a warning sign."
- **Resolution:** EMET preserves "her mouth" (piha, feminine); LOGOS/PNEUMA use "its mouth." The Hebrew is grammatically feminine (ha-arets is feminine), so "her mouth" is more precise, but "its mouth" is standard English for the earth. PNEUMA adds "warning" before "sign" (the Hebrew is simply le-nes, "for a sign"). PNEUMA uses "faction" for edah ("congregation"); SOPHIA retains "congregation." LOGOS's "together with" and PNEUMA's "along with" both render the Hebrew et; SOPHIA adopts "together with." SOPHIA follows LOGOS as the balanced rendering.

### Verse 11
- **EMET:** "And-the-sons of Korah did-not die."
- **LOGOS:** "But the sons of Korah did not die."
- **PNEUMA:** "But the sons of Korah did not die."
- **Resolution:** EMET uses "And" for the Hebrew conjunction; LOGOS/PNEUMA use "But." The Hebrew u-vnei begins with vav-conjunction, which in context carries adversative force. SOPHIA adopts "But" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) as the adversative sense is clear from context.

### Verse 14
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-Simeonite: two and-twenty thousand and-two-hundred."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand and two hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand two hundred."
- **Resolution:** Minor differences: EMET uses singular gentilic; LOGOS/PNEUMA use plural. PNEUMA uses past tense "were." SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The number format differs slightly -- EMET follows the Hebrew word order ("two and twenty"); LOGOS/PNEUMA use standard English order. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 18
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-sons of Gad, by-their-mustered-ones: forty thousand and-five hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the sons of Gad, according to their numbered ones: forty thousand and five hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the descendants of Gad. Their registered number was forty thousand five hundred."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses "descendants" for benei and "registered number" for pequdeihem; splits into two sentences; uses past tense. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's formal rendering.

### Verse 19
- **EMET:** "The-sons of Judah -- Er and-Onan; and-died Er and-Onan in-the-land of Canaan."
- **LOGOS:** "The sons of Judah: Er and Onan. And Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan."
- **PNEUMA:** "The sons of Judah included Er and Onan, but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA adds "included" and uses "but" instead of "And." The Hebrew has a simple vav-consecutive (va-yamot). SOPHIA follows LOGOS's structure, which preserves the Hebrew parataxis.

### Verse 22
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of Judah, by-their-mustered-ones: six and-seventy thousand and-five hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of Judah, according to their numbered ones: seventy-six thousand and five hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of Judah. Their registered number was seventy-six thousand five hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern as other census totals: PNEUMA splits sentences and uses "registered number"; EMET follows Hebrew word order for numbers. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 25
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of Issachar, by-their-mustered-ones: four and-sixty thousand and-three hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of Issachar, according to their numbered ones: sixty-four thousand and three hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of Issachar. Their registered number was sixty-four thousand three hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 27
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-Zebulunite, by-their-mustered-ones: sixty thousand and-five hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the Zebulunites, according to their numbered ones: sixty thousand and five hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the Zebulunites. Their registered number was sixty thousand five hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 29
- **EMET:** "The-sons of Manasseh: of-Machir, the-clan of the-Machirite; and-Machir begot Gilead; of-Gilead, the-clan of the-Gileadite."
- **LOGOS:** "The sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the clan of the Machirites. And Machir begot Gilead; of Gilead, the clan of the Gileadites."
- **PNEUMA:** "The descendants of Manasseh: from Machir, the Machirite clan. And Machir fathered Gilead; from Gilead, the Gileadite clan."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses "descendants," "from," "fathered," and reverses clan name order ("Machirite clan" vs. "clan of the Machirites"). EMET uses "begot" for holid; LOGOS also uses "begot"; PNEUMA uses "fathered." SOPHIA adopts "begot" (EMET/LOGOS) as the more traditional rendering of holid and follows LOGOS's structure.

### Verse 33
- **EMET:** "And-Zelophehad son of Hepher had-not sons but daughters; and-the-names of the-daughters of Zelophehad -- Mahlah and-Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and-Tirzah."
- **LOGOS:** "And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons but only daughters, and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah."
- **PNEUMA:** "Now Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons -- only daughters. And the names of Zelophehad's daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA opens with "Now" instead of "And"; uses "Zelophehad's daughters" (possessive) instead of "daughters of Zelophehad." LOGOS adds "but only daughters" which renders ki im well. SOPHIA follows LOGOS, adding a semicolon before "and the names" for clarity.

### Verse 35
- **EMET:** "These are the-sons of Ephraim by-their-clans: of-Shuthelah, the-clan of the-Shuthelahite; of-Becher, the-clan of the-Bachrite; of-Tahan, the-clan of the-Tahanite."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the sons of Ephraim by their clans: of Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahites; of Becher, the clan of the Becherites; of Tahan, the clan of the Tahanites."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the descendants of Ephraim by their clans: from Shuthelah, the Shuthelahite clan; from Becher, the Becherite clan; from Tahan, the Tahanite clan."
- **Resolution:** EMET uses "Bachrite" for ha-Bakhri; LOGOS uses "Becherites." The gentilic from Becher/Bekher in the Hebrew is ha-Bakhri. LOGOS's "Becherites" is the standard English form. PNEUMA's structural differences (descendants, from, reversed clan order) are noted. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 37
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-sons of Ephraim, by-their-mustered-ones: two and-thirty thousand and-five hundreds. These are the-sons of Joseph by-their-clans."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the sons of Ephraim, according to their numbered ones: thirty-two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph by their clans."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the descendants of Ephraim. Their registered number was thirty-two thousand five hundred. These were the descendants of Joseph by their clans."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern as other census totals. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 41
- **EMET:** "These are the-sons of Benjamin by-their-clans; and-their-mustered-ones -- five and-forty thousand and-six hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the sons of Benjamin by their clans, and their numbered ones were forty-five thousand and six hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the descendants of Benjamin by their clans. Their registered number was forty-five thousand six hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 42
- **EMET:** "These are the-sons of Dan by-their-clans: of-Shuham, the-clan of the-Shuhamite. These are the-clans of Dan by-their-clans."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the sons of Dan by their clans: of Shuham, the clan of the Shuhamites. These are the clans of Dan by their clans."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the descendants of Dan by their clans: from Shuham, the Shuhamite clan. These were the clans of Dan by their clans."
- **Resolution:** Same PNEUMA divergences (descendants, from, reversed clan name, past tense). SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 46
- **EMET:** "And-the-name of the-daughter of Asher -- Serah."
- **LOGOS:** "And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah."
- **PNEUMA:** "And the name of Asher's daughter was Serah."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses possessive "Asher's daughter" instead of "the daughter of Asher." SOPHIA follows LOGOS, preserving the Hebrew construct chain bat-Asher.

### Verse 47
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of the-sons of Asher, by-their-mustered-ones -- three and-fifty thousand and-four hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of the sons of Asher, according to their numbered ones: fifty-three thousand and four hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of the descendants of Asher. Their registered number was fifty-three thousand four hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 50
- **EMET:** "These are the-clans of Naphtali by-their-clans; and-their-mustered-ones -- five and-forty thousand and-four hundreds."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the clans of Naphtali by their clans, and their numbered ones were forty-five thousand and four hundred."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the clans of Naphtali by their clans. Their registered number was forty-five thousand four hundred."
- **Resolution:** Same pattern. SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 51
- **EMET:** "These are the-mustered-ones of the-Sons of Israel: six hundreds thousand and-a-thousand seven hundreds and-thirty."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the numbered ones of the sons of Israel: six hundred and one thousand and seven hundred and thirty."
- **PNEUMA:** "The total number of the Sons of Israel who were registered was six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA paraphrases with "total number... who were registered." EMET's "six hundreds thousand and-a-thousand" is awkward. LOGOS renders the number clearly. SOPHIA follows LOGOS but capitalizes "Sons of Israel" per binding convention.

### Verse 53
- **EMET:** "To-these shall-be-divided the-land as-an-inheritance, by-the-number of names."
- **LOGOS:** "To these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names."
- **PNEUMA:** "The land is to be divided among these tribes as an inheritance, apportioned according to the number of registered names."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA adds "tribes" (not in the Hebrew) and "apportioned" and "registered" (interpretive additions). SOPHIA follows LOGOS, adding a comma after "inheritance" for clarity.

### Verse 54
- **EMET:** "To-the-many you-shall-make-much his-inheritance, and-to-the-few you-shall-make-small his-inheritance; each according-to his-mustered-ones shall-be-given his-inheritance."
- **LOGOS:** "To the greater you shall make greater its inheritance, and to the fewer you shall make fewer its inheritance; each according to its numbered ones shall be given its inheritance."
- **PNEUMA:** "To a larger tribe you are to give a larger inheritance, and to a smaller tribe a smaller inheritance. Each tribe is to receive its inheritance in proportion to its registered number."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA adds "tribe" twice (not in the Hebrew). EMET renders la-rav as "to-the-many" and tarbeh as "you-shall-make-much"; LOGOS renders as "to the greater / make greater." The Hebrew uses the adjectives rav (many/great) and me'at (few/small) as substantives. SOPHIA follows LOGOS but adjusts to "give" instead of "make" for greater naturalness: "To the greater you shall give the greater inheritance, and to the fewer you shall give the fewer inheritance."

### Verse 55
- **EMET:** "Surely by-lot shall-be-divided the-land; by-the-names of the-tribes of their-fathers they-shall-inherit."
- **LOGOS:** "However, by lot the land shall be divided; according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit."
- **PNEUMA:** "The land, however, must be divided by lot. They are to receive their inheritance according to the names of their ancestral tribes."
- **Resolution:** EMET renders akh as "Surely"; LOGOS renders as "However." The Hebrew akh is a restrictive/limitative particle, better rendered "However" (LOGOS) in this context, as it introduces a qualification to v. 54. PNEUMA's "ancestral tribes" replaces "tribes of their fathers." SOPHIA follows LOGOS.

### Verse 56
- **EMET:** "By the-mouth of the-lot shall-be-divided his-inheritance, between the-many and-the-few."
- **LOGOS:** "According to the lot shall its inheritance be divided, between the greater and the fewer."
- **PNEUMA:** "Each inheritance is to be apportioned by lot, whether the tribe is large or small."
- **Resolution:** EMET preserves "the mouth of the lot" (al-pi ha-goral), a vivid personification. LOGOS loses this by rendering "according to the lot." PNEUMA paraphrases entirely. SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's rendering, as "according to the lot" correctly renders al-pi in its idiomatic sense, while preserving formal register.

### Verse 59
- **EMET:** "And-the-name of the-wife of Amram -- Jochebed daughter of Levi, who bore her to-Levi in-Egypt; and-she-bore to-Amram Aaron and-Moses and-Miriam their-sister."
- **LOGOS:** "And the name of the wife of Amram was Jochebed the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bore to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram: Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister."
- **PNEUMA:** "And the name of Amram's wife was Jochebed daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram: Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam."
- **Resolution:** The key divergence is on asher yaldah otah le-Levi be-Mitsrayim. EMET: "who bore her to Levi in Egypt" (ambiguous subject). LOGOS: "whom her mother bore to Levi in Egypt" (supplies "her mother" as subject). PNEUMA: "who was born to Levi in Egypt" (passive construction). The Hebrew is literally "who she-bore her to-Levi in-Egypt" -- the subject is unexpressed and the object otah ("her") refers to Jochebed. LOGOS's addition of "her mother" clarifies the syntax without distorting the meaning. SOPHIA follows LOGOS. Additionally, PNEUMA places "Miriam" before "their sister" and reorders to "their sister Miriam" and uses "Amram's wife" (possessive) instead of "the wife of Amram." SOPHIA preserves "Miriam their sister" as per the Hebrew word order.

### Verse 61
- **EMET:** "And-died Nadab and-Abihu in-their-bringing-near fire strange before YHWH."
- **LOGOS:** "And Nadab and Abihu died when they offered strange fire before YHWH."
- **PNEUMA:** "But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before YHWH."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA uses "But" instead of "And" and renders esh zarah as "unauthorized fire" instead of "strange fire." The Hebrew zarah literally means "strange/foreign/alien." EMET/LOGOS both use "strange fire," which is the traditional and literal rendering. PNEUMA's "unauthorized" is interpretive. SOPHIA adopts "strange fire" (EMET/LOGOS).

### Verse 62
- **EMET:** "And-were their-mustered-ones three and-twenty thousand, every male from-a-son of a-month and-upward; for they-were-not mustered in-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel, for not was-given to-them an-inheritance in-the-midst of the-Sons of Israel."
- **LOGOS:** "And their numbered ones were twenty-three thousand, every male from a month old and upward. For they were not numbered among the sons of Israel, because no inheritance was given to them among the sons of Israel."
- **PNEUMA:** "Their registered number was twenty-three thousand -- every male one month old or more. They were not counted among the Sons of Israel because no inheritance was given to them among the Sons of Israel."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA omits the opening "And"; uses "counted" for hotpaqdu; uses "or more" instead of "and upward." LOGOS uses "because" for the second ki; EMET preserves both ki clauses. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's structure, rendering both ki clauses and using "for" / "because" respectively.

### Verse 63
- **EMET:** "These are the-mustered-ones of Moses and-Eleazar the-priest, who mustered the-Sons of Israel in-the-plains of Moab, by the-Jordan of Jericho."
- **LOGOS:** "These are the ones numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who numbered the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho."
- **PNEUMA:** "These were the ones registered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who counted the Sons of Israel on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan opposite Jericho."
- **Resolution:** EMET: "mustered-ones of Moses" (construct); LOGOS: "ones numbered by Moses" (passive); PNEUMA: "ones registered by Moses." The Hebrew pequdei Mosheh is a construct ("the numbered-ones of Moses"). LOGOS's passive construction conveys this well. PNEUMA uses "on the plains" and "opposite Jericho"; SOPHIA retains "in the plains" and "at Jericho" per LOGOS.

### Verse 64
- **EMET:** "And-among-these there-was-not a-man from-the-mustered-ones of Moses and-Aaron the-priest, who mustered the-Sons of Israel in-the-wilderness of Sinai."
- **LOGOS:** "And among these there was not a man from the numbered ones of Moses and Aaron the priest, who numbered the sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai."
- **PNEUMA:** "And among these there was not a single person who had been counted in the census taken by Moses and Aaron the priest when they counted the Sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai."
- **Resolution:** PNEUMA expands significantly: "single person" for ish ("man"), "who had been counted in the census taken by" restructures the Hebrew, "when they counted" replaces "who numbered." SOPHIA follows LOGOS's closer rendering.

### Verse 65
- **EMET:** "For said YHWH of-them, 'They-shall-surely-die in-the-wilderness.' And-not was-left from-them a-man, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and-Joshua son of Nun."
- **LOGOS:** "For YHWH had said of them, 'They shall surely die in the wilderness.' And there was not left of them a man, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun."
- **PNEUMA:** "For YHWH had said of them, 'They will surely die in the wilderness.' And not one of them survived -- except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun."
- **Resolution:** The infinitive absolute mot yamuthu: EMET/LOGOS render "They shall surely die" (preserving the shall); PNEUMA uses "will surely die." Per binding convention, the infinitive absolute should be preserved with doubling. SOPHIA renders "Dying they shall die" to make the doubling explicit. PNEUMA paraphrases "not one of them survived" for lo' notar mehem ish. LOGOS/EMET are closer to the Hebrew. SOPHIA follows LOGOS but renders the infinitive absolute with explicit doubling.
